behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-hook-input handoff, not just `JSON.parse` in isolation. A Unit test of parser logic could pass while downstream normalization/dispatch mishandles markdown-heavy fields. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, so not System. No AI output dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process hook-input pipeline test with a valid JSON payload whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse succeeds and the next handler receives the expected structured object without error traces.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This requires module wiring (parser + normal handler) and would be under-specified as pure Unit.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on interaction between parse failure detection and trace-record construction/context attachment. Unit-testing only a parser helper misses trace-context formatting and emission contract. Real process/OS I/O is not required by this behavior text, so System is not minimum. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON into the in-process input handler; assert it emits a `json_parse_failed` trace record containing diagnostic context (for example offset/snippet/error message fields) sufficient to locate malformed section.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises parser + tracing module boundary directly.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly local policy mapping: given parse-failure input, return handled result and telemetry intent. This can be caught at one object/function boundary without module wiring. Rejected Integration because text says "local error-policy path," which disqualifies needing cross-module infrastructure for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function/object directly with a representative parse-failure error and assert: (1) no exception escapes, (2) returned result is handled, (3) telemetry-intent flag/enum is set as expected.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions are fully satisfiable at a single boundary; higher level is unnecessary for this behavior.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real subprocess/stdin/exit behavior. In-process fakes can hide crash/exit-order/signal-flush issues. Behavior text explicitly says "real process execution," which disqualifies Integration/Unit. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed bytes/JSON to stdin, close stdin, and assert process exits non-crash path and expected trace signal is emitted before process termination.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires actual subprocess and real stdin/exit semantics.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end real invocation path including stdin and trace artifact emission path; real runtime wiring is the failure boundary. Mocking stdin or sink can miss integration bugs in process I/O and artifact flush/write timing. No model dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and verify the concrete trace artifact from the configured sink (e.g., emitted record/file/stdout trace) contains the expected parse-failure signal.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real invocation path, not just in-process module calls.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is classification/attribution across parser + error-tagging behavior for two input classes. A pure Unit parser test can miss mislabeling in the surrounding handling pipeline. Real OS/subprocess is not required by wording, so System is not minimum. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Add paired in-process regression cases through hook input handling: (A) valid markdown-heavy JSON should not emit parse-failure attribution, (B) malformed JSON should emit parse-failure attribution. Assert labels/signals differ exactly as intended.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Directly tests cross-module attribution behavior with minimal local wiring.
